# Phase 10.0.8 Ethical Survivability Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, autonomous governance, APIs, background jobs, automated moral-authority systems, ethical-scoring enforcement systems, or execution authority.

## Objective

Preserve ethical integrity, constitutional moral boundaries, anti-corruption continuity, institutional moral accountability, and long-horizon ethical legitimacy after the legitimacy and public trust layer.

## In-Scope Documents

- Constitutional ethical-boundary doctrine.
- Governance moral survivability philosophy.
- Anti-corruption continuity doctrine.
- Institutional ethical preservation architecture.
- Constitutional integrity survivability standards.
- Governance ethical-restraint framework.
- Corruption-normalization prevention doctrine.
- Civilization ethical continuity philosophy.
- Institutional moral-accountability standards.
- Governance ethical drift containment doctrine.
- Phase 10.0.8 ethical survivability summary.

## Validation Checklist

- No runtime activation exists.
- No execution authorization exists.
- No deployment authorization exists.
- No provider onboarding exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No infrastructure activation exists.
- No ethical doctrine creates runtime authority.
- No anti-corruption review bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No moral accountability layer enables hidden execution escalation.
- No ethical survivability framework weakens auditability.

## Review Notes

- Ethics remain human-reviewed, auditable, non-runtime, non-operational, and non-self-executing.
- Anti-corruption continuity preserves evidence and warning signs without creating automated enforcement.
- Moral accountability preserves responsibility, uncertainty, dissent, and correction lineage.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, OAuth, APIs, fetch/axios, Prisma/Supabase changes, background jobs, autonomous governance, automated moral-authority systems, ethical-scoring enforcement systems, or execution systems.
